enrichment.short_description: Open-source JavaScript framework for building drag-and-drop visual web builders and template editors.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: GrapesJS is a free, open-source (MIT) framework for building drag-and-drop web builders, with a paid Studio SDK for hosted use.

features.title: Drag-and-drop visual editor
features.description: Core editing engine for building pages, newsletters, and templates without writing markup by hand.

features.title: Custom component system
features.description: Developers can define custom, reusable components and blocks tailored to their own product.

features.title: Asset and layer managers
features.description: Built-in managers for handling images/media and inspecting the page's element hierarchy.

features.title: Clean HTML/CSS/JS export
features.description: Editor state is stored as JSON and can be exported to standard HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

features.title: External data integration
features.description: Supports pulling in external data sources to power dynamic content inside the editor.

features.title: Custom branding and white-label
features.description: Studio SDK plans let companies remove GrapesJS branding and apply their own.

pros: Core framework is free and open source under the MIT license
Can be embedded as a developer framework inside any existing web app
Active GitHub community with 25,000+ stars
Exports clean, standard HTML/CSS/JS with no vendor lock-in
cons: Requires developer setup to embed; not a ready-made builder for non-technical end users on its own
Studio SDK paid plans bill by monthly 'sessions,' which can scale unpredictably for high-traffic products
Removing Studio branding and custom domains requires a paid plan

faqs.question: Is GrapesJS free?
faqs.answer: The core GrapesJS framework is free and open source (MIT license). The hosted Studio SDK adds a free tier plus paid session-based plans starting at $200/month.

faqs.question: What is GrapesJS Studio SDK?
faqs.answer: It's a commercial, hosted layer built on top of the open-source GrapesJS core, adding custom domains, branding removal, and support tiers.

faqs.question: Can I self-host GrapesJS?
faqs.answer: Yes. GrapesJS is an open-source JavaScript library you can embed and host yourself.

faqs.question: Does GrapesJS export standard code?
faqs.answer: Yes, it stores editor state as JSON and can generate HTML, CSS, and JavaScript output.

use_cases.title: White-label page builders
use_cases.description: Embed a drag-and-drop editor directly inside a SaaS product for end users to build pages.

use_cases.title: Email/newsletter editors
use_cases.description: Build a template editor for marketing or transactional email content.

use_cases.title: Custom CMS page builders
use_cases.description: Add visual editing on top of an existing content management workflow.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is GrapesJS?
paragraphs: GrapesJS is an open-source JavaScript framework, licensed under MIT, for building visual, drag-and-drop web builders and template editors. Rather than being a finished end-user product, it's a toolkit that developers embed into their own applications to power page, email, and document editing.

The GrapesJS Studio SDK is a separate commercial layer built on top of the open-source core, offering a free tier plus paid, session-based plans for teams that want hosted infrastructure, custom domains, and custom branding without maintaining that layer themselves.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who GrapesJS is for
paragraphs: GrapesJS is aimed at developers and product teams who need to add a visual editing experience to their own software — for example, a SaaS platform that wants to let its customers design pages, newsletters, or templates without writing HTML.

enrichment.short_description: Visual development platform combining an AI-assisted visual editor, production code, and a headless CMS.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Builder.io lets teams visually edit production code and manage content with AI. Free plan available; paid plans start at $24/user/month.

features.title: Visual editor tied to production code
features.description: Edits made visually map to real components and design tokens instead of exporting disconnected markup.

features.title: Agentic CMS (Publish)
features.description: AI-prompt-driven content creation with A/B testing, personalization, and scheduling.

features.title: Fusion for app/UI development
features.description: Build app and UI code from scratch or from an existing repository.

features.title: Git-integrated collaboration
features.description: Multi-role workflow (design, engineering, PM, marketing) with code review through Git.

features.title: Enterprise integrations
features.description: Connects to commerce, search, asset management, and localization tools, plus an MCP connector.

features.title: Role-based access and SSO
features.description: Available on higher tiers alongside SOC 2 Type II compliance and workflow controls.

pros: Edits sync to real production code rather than exporting static markup
SOC 2 Type II compliant with enterprise security controls
Used by large brands including Everlane, Zapier, and J.Crew
Broad integration surface for commerce, search, and localization tools
cons: Paid plans are priced per user, which can add up for larger teams
AI 'agent credits' are capped per plan; extra credits cost $25 per 500
Enterprise pricing requires contacting sales rather than being self-serve

faqs.question: Does Builder.io have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a Free tier is available at $0/user/month for 1 user with 60 monthly AI agent credits.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Fusion and Publish?
faqs.answer: Fusion is for building apps and UI from scratch or existing repos; Publish is a headless CMS with a visual page builder.

faqs.question: Is Builder.io SOC 2 compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, Builder.io states it is SOC 2 Type II compliant with role-based permissions and workflow controls.

faqs.question: Can Builder.io work with my existing Git workflow?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket, with Azure DevOps and Bitbucket Enterprise on higher tiers.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted landing page publishing
use_cases.description: Marketers create and A/B test pages without needing an engineer for every change.

use_cases.title: Visual UI development on real code
use_cases.description: Engineers and designers collaborate visually while code stays in the team's own repository.

use_cases.title: Headless CMS with personalization
use_cases.description: Enterprises manage content with scheduling and audience targeting at scale.

features: Custom seats and agent credit allocation

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Builder.io?
paragraphs: Builder.io is a visual development platform that lets designers, engineers, product managers, and marketers collaborate on the same product from a single source of truth. Its visual editor maps directly to production code and design tokens rather than generating disconnected output.

The platform is organized around two products: Fusion, for building apps and UI from scratch or an existing repository, and Publish, a headless CMS with a visual page builder, A/B testing, and personalization.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Builder.io is for
paragraphs: Builder.io targets engineering, design, product, and marketing teams at enterprises and scale-ups that need to ship UI and content faster while keeping code under version control and code review.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source visual builder that lets teams design UI visually while integrating with an existing React codebase.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Plasmic is an open-source visual builder for React apps and sites, with a free-forever plan and paid tiers from $39/month.

features.title: Visual drag-and-drop editor
features.description: Build responsive UI visually with layouts that map to real code.

features.title: Figma import
features.description: A Figma-to-code plugin converts existing designs into editable Plasmic components.

features.title: Data integrations
features.description: Connects to Supabase, Contentful, Shopify, HTTP endpoints, and GraphQL APIs.

features.title: Component registration
features.description: Developers register their own React components so non-technical teammates can use them as building blocks.

features.title: Collaboration tools
features.description: Branching, multiplayer editing, and approval workflows for teams.

features.title: Codebase integration
features.description: Integrates with an existing codebase instead of operating as a closed, standalone platform.

pros: Free plan is free forever with no credit card required
Open source: MIT license for most of the codebase, AGPL for the platform directory
Integrates directly with existing React codebases rather than locking teams into a silo
Figma import speeds up design-to-build handoff
cons: Pro ($103/mo) and Scale ($399/mo) tiers, billed yearly, get expensive as teams grow
Removing the Plasmic badge and custom favicon require at least the Starter plan
Enterprise features like SSO and whitelabeling require custom, contact-sales pricing

faqs.question: Is Plasmic free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Plasmic has a free-forever plan for up to 3 collaborators, with paid tiers for more collaborators and features.

faqs.question: Is Plasmic open source?
faqs.answer: Yes. Most of the codebase is MIT licensed, while the platform directory is AGPL licensed.

faqs.question: Can I use my own React components in Plasmic?
faqs.answer: Yes, via component registration, which exposes your existing components as building blocks in the visual editor.

faqs.question: Does Plasmic support importing from Figma?
faqs.answer: Yes, through a dedicated Figma-to-code plugin.

use_cases.title: Visual editing on top of an existing app
use_cases.description: Add a visual builder layer to a product's existing React codebase.

use_cases.title: Marketing pages without developer bottlenecks
use_cases.description: Let marketers build and publish landing pages using components engineers registered.

use_cases.title: Agency client site delivery
use_cases.description: Build client websites with reusable, brandable component libraries.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Plasmic?
paragraphs: Plasmic is an open-source visual editing and content platform for building websites and applications. Rather than acting as a closed, walled-garden builder, it integrates with an existing codebase, letting developers register real React components for use inside the visual editor.
Plasmic offers a free-forever plan alongside paid tiers (Starter, Pro, Scale) billed annually, plus a custom Enterprise tier with SSO and whitelabeling.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Plasmic is for
paragraphs: Plasmic suits development teams that want to give designers, marketers, and product managers a way to build and publish UI visually, without giving up control of the underlying codebase.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, AGPL-licensed visual website builder with full CSS control, self-hosting, or Webstudio's own cloud hosting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Webstudio is an open-source (AGPL) website builder with full CSS control. Free Hobby plan; paid cloud plans start at $15/month.

features.title: Visual CSS editor
features.description: Full access to CSS properties across all breakpoints through a visual interface.

features.title: Design tokens
features.description: Reusable styles for consistency without hand-writing traditional CSS classes.

features.title: CMS integrations
features.description: Connects to Strapi, Supabase, Hygraph, Ghost, Notion, Airtable, WordPress, Contentful, Drupal, Payload, Sanity, Directus, and Decap.

features.title: Dynamic content
features.description: Dynamic routing, data bindings, collection lists, and dynamic sitemaps.

features.title: Automatic image optimization
features.description: Converts images to WebP/AVIF with compression automatically.

features.title: Cloud or self-hosted publishing
features.description: One-click publishing via Cloudflare, or export and self-host the project independently.

pros: Core builder is open source under AGPL-3.0 and can be self-hosted
Free Hobby plan offers unlimited projects and pages on a wstd.io subdomain
Sites exported from Webstudio are the operator's own code, not bound by AGPL
Wide range of native CMS integrations
cons: Custom domains and higher page-view limits require the paid Pro plan
Overage charges of $20 per 100,000 additional page views apply on the Pro plan
Some optional components, like the animation SDK package, are proprietary rather than open source

faqs.question: Is Webstudio free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Hobby plan is free, offering unlimited projects and pages on a wstd.io subdomain.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Webstudio?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core builder is open source under AGPL-3.0-or-later and can be self-hosted.

faqs.question: Does Webstudio support custom domains?
faqs.answer: Yes, starting on the paid Pro plan ($15/month billed yearly).

faqs.question: What CMS platforms does Webstudio integrate with?
faqs.answer: Strapi, Supabase, Hygraph, Ghost, Notion, Airtable, WordPress, Contentful, Drupal, Payload, Sanity, Directus, and Decap.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted Webflow alternative
use_cases.description: Teams that want a visual builder they can host on their own infrastructure.

use_cases.title: CMS-driven marketing sites
use_cases.description: Startups building marketing pages backed by a headless CMS.

use_cases.title: Full CSS control without hand-coding
use_cases.description: Designers who need pixel-level CSS access via a visual interface.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Webstudio?
paragraphs: Webstudio is an open-source website builder, licensed under AGPL-3.0-or-later, that gives designers full access to CSS properties through a visual interface rather than a limited style panel. Sites can be published through Webstudio's Cloudflare-backed cloud hosting or exported and self-hosted independently.
The platform reports 229,800+ projects created and 140,200+ users, with 8,700+ GitHub stars on its open-source repository.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Webstudio is for
paragraphs: Webstudio is built for designers, developers, and startup founders looking for an alternative to closed no-code builders like Webflow and Framer, particularly those who value self-hosting or full CSS control.

enrichment.short_description: Low-code website builder combining AI page generation with multi-framework code export and headless CMS integration.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: TeleportHQ is an AI-assisted, low-code website builder that exports multi-framework code. 7-day free trial; plans from $9/editor/month.

features.title: AI page generation
features.description: Generates page layouts from text prompts describing the desired design.

features.title: Multi-framework code export
features.description: Real-time code generation across multiple JavaScript frameworks, plus HTML/CSS/JS export.

features.title: Figma-to-code plugin
features.description: Converts existing Figma designs into TeleportHQ projects.

features.title: Headless CMS and data sources
features.description: Connects external data sources and headless CMS content into built pages.

features.title: GitHub integration
features.description: Version control integration for exported code.

features.title: Built-in form management
features.description: Form creation with lead collection and submission limits per plan.

features.title: One-click Vercel deployment
features.description: Deploy published sites directly to Vercel.

pros: Exports real, framework-specific code instead of locking users into a proprietary runtime
GitHub and Vercel integrations fit standard developer workflows
Figma import accelerates design-to-code handoff
Annual billing roughly halves the Professional plan's per-editor price
cons: No indefinite free plan — only a 7-day free trial before a paid plan is required
AI token allowance is capped per plan (15k on trial, 75k/month on Professional)
Agency-tier pricing requires contacting sales
Hosting bandwidth and storage are capped even on the paid Professional plan

faqs.question: Does TeleportHQ have a free plan?
faqs.answer: It offers a 7-day free trial; continued use requires the Professional plan, starting at $9/editor/month billed yearly or $18/editor/month billed monthly.

faqs.question: What code does TeleportHQ export?
faqs.answer: Real-time code across multiple JavaScript frameworks, along with HTML/CSS/JS export.

faqs.question: Can TeleportHQ generate pages with AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, via prompt-based AI page generation with a monthly AI token allowance.

faqs.question: Does TeleportHQ integrate with version control?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with GitHub.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted client site delivery
use_cases.description: Agencies turn mockups into working, framework-specific code quickly.

use_cases.title: Framework-specific code export from a visual builder
use_cases.description: Developers need exported code that matches their target JS framework.

use_cases.title: Landing pages with lead capture
use_cases.description: Marketers build pages with built-in form submission tracking.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is TeleportHQ?
paragraphs: TeleportHQ is a low-code website builder that pairs a visual, drag-and-drop editor with AI-driven page generation and real-time code generation across multiple JavaScript frameworks. Projects can pull in data sources, connect to headless CMS content, and version through GitHub.
New users get a 7-day free trial; ongoing use requires the Professional plan or a custom Agency plan for larger organizations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who TeleportHQ is for
paragraphs: TeleportHQ targets designers, developers, marketers, agencies, and freelancers who want to move quickly from an idea or Figma design to deployable, framework-specific code.

enrichment.short_description: Desktop visual editor for professional developers that works with real HTML/CSS, Bootstrap, Tailwind, and WordPress themes.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pinegrow is a desktop visual web editor for HTML, CSS, Tailwind, and WordPress themes, priced from $99/year or as a one-time license.

features.title: Visual and code editing
features.description: Combines drag-and-drop tools with direct HTML and CSS code editors.

features.title: Framework support
features.description: Built-in components and visual controls for Bootstrap, Foundation, and Tailwind CSS.

features.title: Responsive multi-page editing
features.description: Edit multiple pages at different device sizes with media query helpers.

features.title: WordPress theme builder
features.description: Dedicated add-on for building custom WordPress themes and plugin support.

features.title: GSAP-powered animation
features.description: Interaction composer and timeline editor built on GreenSock GSAP.

features.title: Smart components
features.description: Master pages and reusable components with static CMS capabilities.

features.title: AI assistant
features.description: Built-in AI assistant for design and code help, using the user's own API key.

pros: Works directly with real HTML/CSS files rather than a proprietary format, fitting version-controlled workflows
Offers a one-time perpetual license option, not just a subscription
Dedicated WordPress theme-building tooling
Cross-platform desktop app for Mac, Windows, and Linux
30-day money-back guarantee on annual and one-time purchases
cons: Core interactions, Tailwind editor, WordPress builder, and WooCommerce tooling are separate paid add-ons
AI assistant requires bringing your own API key, so usage has separate external cost
Desktop-only; there is no hosted, collaborative cloud version

faqs.question: Is Pinegrow a subscription?
faqs.answer: Pinegrow offers both an annual subscription and a one-time perpetual license (which includes one year of free updates).

faqs.question: Does Pinegrow work with WordPress?
faqs.answer: Yes, via a dedicated WordPress theme builder add-on.

faqs.question: What platforms does Pinegrow support?
faqs.answer: Mac (Apple Silicon and Intel), Windows 64-bit, and Linux 64-bit.

faqs.question: Does Pinegrow include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, a built-in AI assistant that uses your own API key.

use_cases.title: WordPress theme development
use_cases.description: Build custom WordPress themes visually with a dedicated theme builder add-on.

use_cases.title: Visual CSS/Tailwind tooling alongside hand-written code
use_cases.description: Professional developers who want visual controls without giving up code ownership.

use_cases.title: Offline, code-owned site building
use_cases.description: Teams that need to build websites without depending on cloud infrastructure.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pinegrow?
paragraphs: Pinegrow is a desktop application for visual web development that operates directly on standard HTML and CSS files, combining drag-and-drop visual tools with code editors. It includes built-in support for Bootstrap, Foundation, and Tailwind CSS, plus a WordPress theme builder add-on.

It's sold as either an annual subscription or a one-time perpetual license with one year of included updates, with add-ons like Interactions, Tailwind Editor, WordPress Builder, and WooCommerce priced separately.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pinegrow is for
paragraphs: Pinegrow targets professional web developers and designers who want visual tooling that integrates with their existing code-based workflows and version control, rather than a cloud-dependent, all-in-one builder.

enrichment.short_description: Desktop app for visually building responsive Bootstrap websites, sold as a one-time purchase with no subscription.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Bootstrap Studio is a desktop visual website builder for Bootstrap sites, sold as a one-time purchase from $29, no subscription.

features.title: Visual drag-and-drop editor
features.description: Build responsive websites using a Bootstrap 3/4/5 component library without writing code.

features.title: Built-in AI assistant
features.description: Used for edits, complete website builds, and redesigns.

features.title: Code editor
features.description: VS Code-like environment for CSS, SASS, JavaScript, and HTML editing.

features.title: Animations
features.description: Scroll/hover-triggered CSS animations, Lottie support, and parallax effects.

features.title: Smart Forms and ecommerce components
features.description: Forms with automatic email delivery, plus built-in ecommerce components.

features.title: One-click SFTP publishing
features.description: Publish directly via SFTP, with free hosting slots on Bootstrap Studio's platform.

features.title: Import existing sites
features.description: Drag and drop existing HTML, CSS, and JS files to bring a site into the editor.

pros: One-time payment; no recurring subscription required
Standard plan includes 1 year of free upgrades; Lifetime plan includes upgrades and hosting forever
30-day refund policy on both plans
Cross-platform desktop app for Windows, macOS, and Linux
cons: Desktop-only; there is no browser-based or cloud editing option
Free hosting and SFTP publishing are app-provided rather than a full managed hosting platform
Standard plan's free upgrades are limited to 1 year

faqs.question: Is Bootstrap Studio a subscription?
faqs.answer: No, it's a one-time purchase: $29 for the Standard plan or $59 for the Lifetime plan.

faqs.question: What operating systems does Bootstrap Studio support?
faqs.answer: Windows 10+, macOS, and Linux.

faqs.question: Does Bootstrap Studio include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, a built-in AI assistant for edits and full website builds.

faqs.question: Can I import an existing website?
faqs.answer: Yes, by dragging and dropping existing HTML, CSS, and JS files into the app.

use_cases.title: Client site delivery
use_cases.description: Freelancers and agencies build client sites using the Bootstrap component library.

use_cases.title: Learning web design visually
use_cases.description: Beginners build sites without writing CSS from scratch.

use_cases.title: Desktop visual building with full code access
use_cases.description: Developers who want visual tools without losing access to raw code.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Bootstrap Studio?
paragraphs: Bootstrap Studio is a desktop application for visually designing and publishing websites built on the Bootstrap framework (versions 3, 4, and 5), generating static HTML output. It runs locally rather than in the browser and includes a built-in AI assistant, a code editor, and one-click SFTP publishing.
It's sold as a one-time purchase — $29 for the Standard plan with 1 year of free upgrades, or $59 for a Lifetime plan with lifetime upgrades and hosting — rather than a subscription.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Bootstrap Studio is for
paragraphs: Bootstrap Studio serves a range of users from beginners unfamiliar with CSS to experienced frontend developers, including freelancers, agencies, and students building Bootstrap-based websites.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source (AGPL) visual builder for static sites, maintained by the nonprofit Silex Labs.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Silex is a 100% free, open-source (AGPL) visual builder for static sites with CMS integrations, maintained by Silex Labs.

features.title: Visual drag-and-drop editor
features.description: Build sites visually with direct access to underlying HTML and CSS.

features.title: CMS integrations
features.description: Connects to WordPress, Strapi, Supabase, Squidex, and GraphQL APIs.

features.title: Static site generation with dynamic data
features.description: Generates static output while still supporting dynamic, data-driven content.

features.title: SEO-optimized output
features.description: Generated sites are designed to be SEO-friendly and accessible by default.

features.title: No vendor lock-in
features.description: Built on open web standards so exported sites aren't tied to Silex.

features.title: Offline-first desktop app
features.description: A desktop app with offline-first capabilities is in development.

pros: 100% free and open source under the AGPL license, with no paid tiers
Maintained by Silex Labs, a nonprofit organization
No proprietary lock-in; produces standard HTML/CSS output
Received the OW2 Best Project Award 2026
cons: Funded by donations, which can mean slower feature development than VC-backed competitors
The offline-first desktop app is still in development and not yet available
Smaller plugin/integration ecosystem than commercial alternatives like Webflow

faqs.question: Is Silex really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, all features are free of charge. The project is funded through donations rather than subscriptions.

faqs.question: Who maintains Silex?
faqs.answer: Silex Labs, a nonprofit organization.

faqs.question: What license is Silex released under?
faqs.answer: The AGPL license.

faqs.question: Can Silex connect to a CMS?
faqs.answer: Yes, including WordPress, Strapi, Supabase, and Squidex.

use_cases.title: Free alternative to proprietary builders
use_cases.description: Freelancers and agencies wanting a code-owned, no-cost alternative to platforms like Webflow.

use_cases.title: Learning web design
use_cases.description: Students building sites visually while learning underlying HTML/CSS concepts.

use_cases.title: CMS-driven static sites
use_cases.description: Nonprofits and individuals building free sites backed by dynamic data sources.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Silex?
paragraphs: Silex is a visual builder for static sites that combines a visual editor, CMS integrations (including WordPress, Strapi, and Supabase), and hosting-agnostic static output. It's fully open source under the AGPL license and maintained by the nonprofit Silex Labs.
All features are free of charge; the project sustains development through donations and community contributions rather than paid subscription tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Silex is for
paragraphs: Silex is aimed at web professionals, freelancers, agencies, and students who want a free, open-source alternative to proprietary site builders while retaining full control over code and hosting.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source static site generator written in Go, known for very fast site builds.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Hugo is a free, open-source (Apache 2.0) static site generator written in Go, built for extremely fast site rendering.

features.title: Fast rendering
features.description: Renders large sites in seconds, according to the project's own performance claims.

features.title: Advanced templating
features.description: Flexible templating system for controlling site structure and design.

features.title: Built-in asset pipeline
features.description: Image processing, JavaScript bundling, Sass support, and Tailwind CSS integration.

features.title: Built-in development server
features.description: Local server with real-time preview during development.

features.title: Multilingual support
features.description: Handles multiple language versions of a site.

features.title: Taxonomy system
features.description: Organizes content with categories, tags, and custom taxonomies.

pros: Completely free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license
Written in Go for high build performance
89,000+ GitHub stars with an active community
Outputs static files with no database or server runtime required
cons: No built-in visual editor; content is authored via templates, Markdown, and the command line
Templating language has a steeper learning curve than simpler generators
No official hosted/managed version; requires self-hosting or a static hosting provider

faqs.question: Is Hugo free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Hugo is free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license.

faqs.question: What language is Hugo built in?
faqs.answer: Go.

faqs.question: Does Hugo require a database?
faqs.answer: No, it generates static files with no database or server-side runtime.

faqs.question: Does Hugo support multiple languages?
faqs.answer: Yes, multilingual site support is built in.

use_cases.title: Documentation sites
use_cases.description: Fast builds suit large, frequently updated documentation.

use_cases.title: Marketing and informational sites
use_cases.description: Simple, fast static sites for organizations and nonprofits.

use_cases.title: Blogs and news publishing
use_cases.description: A code-based, database-free publishing workflow.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Hugo?
paragraphs: Hugo is a popular open-source static site generator written in Go, designed to render sites — including large ones — in seconds. It's released under the Apache 2.0 license and includes advanced templating, a built-in asset pipeline for images, JS, and Sass, and multilingual support.
The project has an active open-source community, with 89,000+ GitHub stars and a rapid release cycle.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Hugo is for
paragraphs: Hugo suits developers and technical teams building documentation sites, landing pages, corporate and government sites, nonprofit sites, educational sites, news outlets, and project sites who need fast builds and no database dependency.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source Ruby-based static site generator with built-in blogging support and native GitHub Pages hosting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Jekyll is a free, open-source (MIT) Ruby static site generator with built-in blogging support, powering GitHub Pages.

features.title: Markdown and Liquid support
features.description: Converts Markdown, Liquid, HTML, and CSS content into a static site.

features.title: Blog-aware structure
features.description: Built-in permalinks, categories, pages, posts, and custom layouts.

features.title: No database required
features.description: Content-first workflow with no database or comment moderation system to manage.

features.title: Command-line deployment
features.description: Sites can be built and deployed from the command line in seconds.

features.title: Native GitHub Pages integration
features.description: GitHub Pages is powered by Jekyll, giving many users free hosting with no extra setup.

features.title: Custom domain support
features.description: Supports serving sites from a custom domain name.

pros: Free and open source under the MIT license
Powers GitHub Pages, giving many users free hosting with minimal configuration
Simple, content-first workflow with no database to manage
Maintained by a volunteer core team with sponsor support
cons: Requires Ruby and gem installation, adding setup friction for non-Ruby developers
No visual/WYSIWYG editor; content is authored via Markdown/Liquid and the command line
Maintained by a volunteer core team rather than a dedicated company

faqs.question: Is Jekyll free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jekyll is free and open source under the MIT license.

faqs.question: What language is Jekyll built with?
faqs.answer: Ruby.

faqs.question: Does Jekyll require a database?
faqs.answer: No, no databases or comment moderation systems are required.

faqs.question: Can I host a Jekyll site for free?
faqs.answer: Yes, GitHub Pages is powered by Jekyll and offers free hosting for Jekyll sites.

use_cases.title: Free blogging on GitHub Pages
use_cases.description: Developers publish a blog directly through GitHub Pages at no cost.

use_cases.title: Documentation without a database backend
use_cases.description: Technical teams publish docs using a simple, file-based workflow.

use_cases.title: Lightweight personal blogging
use_cases.description: Individuals wanting full content ownership with a simple static site.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Jekyll?
paragraphs: Jekyll is a static site generator that converts plain text — written in Markdown, Liquid, HTML, and CSS — into websites and blogs, with no database or comment moderation system required. It's released under the MIT license and can be deployed from the command line in seconds.
Jekyll is the engine behind GitHub Pages, which gives many Jekyll users free hosting with minimal configuration, alongside support for custom domains.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Jekyll is for
paragraphs: Jekyll is aimed at developers and content creators who want a lightweight, blog-aware static site generator without the overhead of managing a hosting company or database, particularly those already using GitHub.
